nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: 印度
nodes.summary: 印度是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联317种鸟类，包括青头潜鸭、环颈山鹧鸪、红胸山鹧鸪等317种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 气候变暖
nodes.summary: 长期升温改变海冰和海洋环境条件。

nodes.name: 黑斑蝗莺
nodes.summary: 黑斑蝗莺（Common grasshopper warbler，学名 Locustella naevia），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，主要分布于印度、斯里蘭卡，常见栖息地包括稀树草原，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、气候变暖。

nodes.name: 斯里蘭卡
nodes.summary: 斯里蘭卡是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联59种鸟类，包括蓝孔雀、Painted francolin、大红鹳等59种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: In autumn, it migrates south to the Sahel region of tropical Africa, India and Sri Lanka where it overwinters. In the breeding season, the grasshopper warbler is found in damp or dry places with rough grass and bushes such as the edges of fens, clearings, neglected hedgerows, heaths, upland moors, gorse-covered areas, young plantations and felled woodland

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: In autumn, it migrates south to the Sahel region of tropical Africa, India and Sri Lanka where it overwinters. In the breeding season, the grasshopper warbler is found in damp or dry places with rough grass and bushes such as the edges of fens, clearings, neglected hedgerows, heaths, upland moors, gorse-covered areas, young plantations and felled woodland

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: This small passerine bird is found in dense grassland vegetation, often close to water, and usually with a few small scattered shrubs

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The total number of birds may be on the decline because of habitat loss, but not to an extent that would warrant listing the bird under a higher risk category. In a study examining the possible effects of global warming on the range of various species of bird, it was estimated that the breeding range of the common grasshopper warbler would be displaced several hundred miles northwards and would cover the British Isles and the whole of Scandinavia but that it would cease to breed in much of its present range in mainland Europe.{{cite journal |author1=Huntley, Brian |author2=Collingham, Yvonne C
